tasks.title: Analyze ClaudeCode Competitor
tasks.description: Review the source code in ../claude-code-sourcemap to identify key features and capabilities of ClaudeCode. Document the findings including unique functionalities, user interface elements, and technical implementations that could be valuable additions to our product.

tasks.title: Analyze Copilot-CLI Competitor
tasks.description: Research and analyze the copilot-cli product to understand its features, capabilities, and user experience. Document key functionalities, command-line interface patterns, and any innovative approaches that could enhance our product.

tasks.title: Analyze OpenCode Competitor
tasks.description: Examine the source code in ../opencode to identify core features and architectural decisions of OpenCode. Document significant functionalities, development workflows, and technical implementations that might benefit our product.

tasks.title: Analyze Roocode Competitor
tasks.description: Investigate the roocode product to understand its feature set, user experience, and technical approach. Document notable capabilities, interface designs, and implementation strategies that could be incorporated into our solution.

tasks.title: Product Review and Gap Analysis
tasks.description: Conduct a comprehensive review of our current product to identify existing features and limitations. Compare against documented competitor analyses to determine gaps and opportunities for improvement.

tasks.title: Create SWARMOUT2.md Feature Recommendations
tasks.description: Based on all competitor analyses and product review, create a document called SWARMOUT2.md in target/temp/swarmout/ directory. Include detailed feature suggestions, implementation priorities, and rationale for each proposed addition.

tasks.title: Generate Implementation Plan with Milestones
tasks.description: Create a detailed implementation plan within SWARMOUT2.md that includes specific milestones, tasks, estimated effort, and dependencies for implementing all recommended features. Organize by priority and logical development sequence.
